title: gardenia rutenbergiana subsp. rutenbergiana
intro: Gardenia rutenbergiana subsp. rutenbergiana, also known as Rutenbergia gardenioides, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the coastal forests of Kenya and Tanzania, where its flowers are highly prized for their fragrance and medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Gardenia rutenbergiana subsp. rutenbergiana have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its health benefits, which include reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
